result.meeting_context: This is a Joint Administrative, Zoning and Subdivision Committee hearing conducted on November 9th via video conference during a declared state of emergency. The meeting addressed three development projects requiring permit modifications, extensions, and approvals in Mountain View.

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: Meeting conducted via video conference with no physical location during declared state of emergency, following California Government Code Section 54953E.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : This meeting will be conducted in accordance with the California Government Code Section 5, 4953 E as authorized by resolution

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: All committee members present: Nicole Wright (City Attorney's office), Ed Arango (Public Works), and Lindsay Hagan (Assistant Community Development Director acting as zoning administrator).
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : So I will start with Nicole Wright from the City Attorney's office present

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: 350 Ellis Street planned community permit modification for office campus on 19.6 acre site continued from October 26th hearing and will be re-noticed when ready.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : This item will not be heard at this meeting and was previously continued from the October 26th Zoning Administrator hearing

points.speaker: Aki Snelling
points.text: Trinity United Methodist Church requests extending cold weather shelter hours from 3:00 PM to 9:00 AM (currently 4:00 PM to 6:30 AM) to align with school hours for families with children.
points.source: Aki Snelling : The church and home for services are requesting to extend the hours of operation to begin at 3:00 PM rather than the current 4:00 PM allowed, and to end at 9:00 AM rather than the existing 6:30 AM daily

points.speaker: Aki Snelling
points.text: Shelter operates annually during cold weather months from Thanksgiving week through March 31st for approximately 3.5 months, serving up to 50 women and families maximum.
points.source: Aki Snelling : The shelter only operates during the cold weather months starting think the week of Thanksgiving through March 31st of the following year

points.speaker: Aki Snelling
points.text: Community meeting held with Old Mountain View Neighborhood Association on October 17th resulted in no concerns and full support from all speakers.
points.source: Aki Snelling : A community meeting was also held with the Old Mountain View Neighborhood Association on October 17th. No speakers voiced any concerns, and all who spoke were in support of the project

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: Lindsay Hagan approved the provisional use permit modification with staff-recommended conditions, finding the extended hours appropriate for aligning with school schedules.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : And so with that, I do make a motion to approve this item with the conditions that have been included in the findings report

points.speaker: Eric Anderson
points.text: Marwood Company requests two-year extension for four-story commercial building with 28,090 square feet office and 6,500 square feet retail on 0.25 acre site.
points.source: Eric Anderson : The applicant is Marwood Company

points.speaker: Eric Anderson
points.text: Staff recommends approval based on applicant's work preparing modifications consistent with Downtown Parking Strategy adopted by City Council on November 9th, 2021.
points.source: Eric Anderson : staff's recommending approval of this extension based on the work that the applicant has done to prepare and submit proposed modifications to the project consistent with the Downtown Parking Strategy which was adopted by the City Council on November 9th, 2021

points.speaker: Wing Tam
points.text: Norcal Carpenter Union Local 405 representative requested developer use prevailing wage standards, healthcare benefits, and local hiring for the project.
points.source: Wing Tam : Today I would like to speaking in here and spit out our provenant area standards labor

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: Lindsay Hagan approved the two-year extension with staff conditions, finding it materially the same project with minor condition tweaks.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : And so with that I do make a motion to approve this with the conditions as drafted by staff and posted online

points.speaker: Jeffrey Tsumura
points.text: Emerick McDonald for Octane Fiat LLC requests one-year extension for 44-unit condominium project with state density bonus, replacing 6 dwelling units and commercial building on 0.67 acre site.
points.source: Jeffrey Tsumura : the applicant is Emerick McDonald for Octane Fiat LLC

points.speaker: Jeffrey Tsumura
points.text: Project originally approved November 17th, 2020; applicant has prepared comprehensive building permit submittal and expects to submit construction documents in coming weeks.
points.source: Jeffrey Tsumura : The project was approved back in November 17th, 2020 and since then the applicant has worked diligently to prepare a comprehensive set for building permit submittal

points.speaker: Ed Arango
points.text: Ed Arango motioned to approve one-year extension of vesting tentative map subject to conditions of approval in conformance with General Plan and zoning ordinance.
points.source: Ed Arango : I'd like to make a motion to approve the one year extension of their previously approved vesting tentative map

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: Committee approved both the vesting tentative map extension and related permits (planned community permit, development review permit, heritage tree removal permit) with staff conditions.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : And so on that note I do make a motion to approve that item and so with that this approval has passed

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: All approved items have 10-day appeal periods, with follow-up questions directed to respective project planners (Aki Snelling, Eric Anderson, Jeffrey Tsumura).
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : there is a 10 calendar day appeal. Should there be anyone interested in appealing this item

points.speaker: Lindsay Hagan
points.text: Joint Administrative, Zoning and Subdivision Committee hearing concluded at 4:22 PM on November 9th.
points.source: Lindsay Hagan : that will conclude our Joint Administrative, Zoning and Subdivision Committee public hearing of November 9th at 4:22

decisions.text: Lindsay Hagan approved provisional use permit modification for 748 Mercy Street cold weather shelter to extend hours from 3:00 PM to 9:00 AM with staff conditions.
decisions.source: Lindsay Hagan : And so with that, I do make a motion to approve this item with the conditions that have been included in the findings report

decisions.text: Lindsay Hagan approved two-year extension for 1747 West Evelyn Avenue commercial building project with staff-drafted conditions.
decisions.source: Lindsay Hagan : And so with that I do make a motion to approve this with the conditions as drafted by staff and posted online

decisions.text: Ed Arango motioned to approve one-year extension of vesting tentative map for 2645-2655 Fayette Drive condominium project subject to conditions of approval.
decisions.source: Ed Arango : I'd like to make a motion to approve the one year extension of their previously approved vesting tentative map

decisions.text: Lindsay Hagan approved related permits (planned community permit, development review permit, heritage tree removal permit) for Fayette Drive project with staff conditions.
decisions.source: Lindsay Hagan : And so on that note I do make a motion to approve that item and so with that this approval has passed
